title: validatekey
text: validatekey Validate a key of the current occurrence. Explain this code validatekey Entity {,KeyNumber} Copy Example: validatekey "MYENT", 1 Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter DataType Description Entity String Entity in which a key is to be validated. KeyNumber Number Key that is to be located (as defined for Entity ; whole (integer) number; the value will be truncated to form an integer. If omitted, the default value of 1 (the primary key) is chosen. Higher KeyNumbers (2, 3, 4, and so on) identify a candidate key depending upon your model definition. Indexes are not allowed. Return Values Table 2. Values returned in $status Value Meaning <0 An error occurred. $procerror contains the exact error. >=0 Statement executed successfully. Use Allowed in all component types, except self-contained Reports. Description The validatekey statement validates the specified key of the current occurrence of Entity . If any field of the key is marked as modified and has not been successfully validated, the necessary field-level validate triggers and the validatekey trigger are activated. For more information, see the "Validate" topic. As each field of the key is successfully validated, the corresponding validation flag ( $fieldvalidation ) is set to 0. When the entire key is successfully validated, $keyvalidation is set to 0. Note: Validation statements ( validatefield , validatekey , validateocc , and validate ) activate the validation triggers such as validate and validatekey. You should therefore be careful when using these statements in validation triggers. Example: Using validatekey The following operation validates a new primary key that it receives as an argument: Explain this code operation NEWKEYCHECK params string NEWKEY : IN boolean OK : OUT endparams PK1.MYENT = NEWKEY validatekey "MYENT", 1 if ($status = 0) OK = <TRUE> else OK = <FALSE> endif end; NEWKEYCHECK Copy Related Links Validate Determines whether the valerr data processing attribute is added for the selected entity node in the DTD tree. This attribute holds the occurrence or field validation error (depending on whether the selected item is an entity or field). Valid values are True or False (default). validatefield Validate a field of the current occurrence. validateocc Validate an occurrence. $dataerrorcontext Return the context of the last validation error. $procerrorcontext Return the location of the error specified by $procerror. $keyvalidation Identify whether a primary or candidate key requires validation. $fieldvalidation Identify whether a field requires validation. trigger validate (Field) System trigger that defines the procedural checks to validate a modified field. trigger validateKey System trigger that defines the procedural checks to validate a modified primary or candidate key. trigger validate (Entity) System trigger for validating entity occurrences.
